title: The Glitch in the Family Photo
idea: The creator finds a 1990s polaroid of their parents at a restaurant, but there is a person in the background wearing modern tech (like wireless earbuds or a smartwatch) that didn't exist in 1994. The video zooms in on the person's face, which appears blurred or distorted in a way that suggests digital manipulation, even though the photo is a physical analog print. The creator asks the audience to help identify the object or the person, leading to a hunt for the location of the restaurant to see if there are other 'glitch' photos from that area.
hook: I was going through my parents' old wedding photos when I found something that literally makes no sense.
targetAudience: True crime enthusiasts, fans of paranormal/glitch-in-the-matrix content, and internet sleuths.
viralAngle: It combines nostalgia with a 'glitch' element that forces viewers to rewatch and zoom in to verify if the object is real, creating a 'comment bait' scenario where users will argue about whether it's a Photoshop hoax or genuine proof of a time slip.
